title: validate verb + ajv shipped as a real dependency
description: Gate agents and clerk validate work-tracking JSON against .excn/schemas/ with ajv, but the package ships no validator, so every agent ad-hoc-installs ajv into the host (and npm prunes those modules on the next install — observed on the dogfood root 2026-06-11). Ship `to-execution validate <file> [--schema <path>]` that auto-detects the schema from the file's location/shape (issues partitions, sprint, PRD, progress, hooks config, verdict ledger) and validates with ajv; add ajv (+ ajv-formats if needed) to src/package.json dependencies so the npm install carries it. Exit 0 on valid, non-zero with violation listing on invalid (fail-closed CLI rule).
acceptance_criteria: ajv is a dependency in src/package.json; npm-installed package validates without any ad-hoc install.
`to-execution validate` auto-detects the right schema for backlog/sprint-issues, sprint, PRD, progress, hooks-config, and verdict-ledger files; --schema overrides.
Valid file exits 0; invalid exits non-zero listing each violation with JSON path.
Usage/help text updated; passes code-standards and package-qa gates.
Gate-agent/clerk guidance (CONTEXT or TEAM_DIRECTIVE) points at the verb instead of ad-hoc ajv installs.
root_cause: Package shipped no validator, so gate agents ad-hoc-installed ajv into hosts; the next npm install pruned those modules.
fix: validate verb auto-detects the .excn schema by location/shape and validates with ajv, now a real dependency; TEAM_DIRECTIVE points gates/clerk at the verb. Commit 487cdcf; gates PASS incl. alignment on the directive edit.
